manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: honey, draft, scientist, flute, jet, match, glove, washington, nail.
Your opponent's team words are: ring, buck, mercury, post, saturn, tag, face, mole.
Distractor words are: capital, force, opera, tooth, ship, degree, sub.
The kill words are: plastic.

manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

TARGETS: <TARGETS>

manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: honey, draft, scientist, flute, jet, match, glove, washington, nail.
Your opponent's team words are: ring, buck, mercury, post, saturn, tag, face, mole.
Distractor words are: capital, force, opera, tooth, ship, degree, sub.
The kill words are: plastic.

CLUE: STREAM
TARGETS: jet, honey, draft

The words jet, draft were guessed correctly. The word mercury was guessed but is an opponent word. Your teammate's turn ended there.
Now provide another clue relating to some of your remaining team words and a list of the related target words. Remember to start your clue with 'CLUE: ', put a new line, and start your comma-separated list of target words with 'TARGETS: '. Notice: some words have been removed from the lists compared to previous requests.

Your remaining team words are: honey, scientist, flute, match, glove, washington, nail.
Remaining words for your opponent are: ring, buck, post, saturn, tag, face, mole.
Remaining distractor words are: capital, force, opera, tooth, ship, degree, sub.
Remaining kill words are: plastic.

CLUE: GEORGE
TARGETS: Washington, Scientist

The word washington was guessed correctly. The word capital was guessed but is an innocent word. Your teammate's turn ended there.
Now provide another clue relating to some of your remaining team words and a list of the related target words. Remember to start your clue with 'CLUE: ', put a new line, and start your comma-separated list of target words with 'TARGETS: '. Notice: some words have been removed from the lists compared to previous requests.

Your remaining team words are: honey, scientist, flute, match, glove, nail.
Remaining words for your opponent are: ring, buck, post, saturn, tag, face, mole.
Remaining distractor words are: force, opera, tooth, ship, degree, sub.
Remaining kill words are: plastic.

CLUE: LAB
TARGETS: scientist, glove

The words scientist, glove were guessed correctly. Your teammate's turn ended there.
Now provide another clue relating to some of your remaining team words and a list of the related target words. Remember to start your clue with 'CLUE: ', put a new line, and start your comma-separated list of target words with 'TARGETS: '. Notice: some words have been removed from the lists compared to previous requests.

Remaining words for your opponent are: ring, buck, post, saturn, tag, face, mole.
Remaining distractor words are: force, opera, tooth, ship, degree, sub.
Remaining kill words are: plastic.

Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Thinking Process:

1.  **Analyze the Request:**
    *   Task: Provide a comma-separated list of 1 to 2 words from a given list that relate to the target word 'polish'.
    *   Constraint 1: Start the answer with 'GUESS: '.
    *   Constraint 2: Do not include any other text.
    *   Context: This is a game of Codenames (or similar association game). Previous turns have revealed information about other words (stream, george, lab, box).
    *   Available Words: mole, nail, opera, saturn, sub, tag, plastic, degree, flute, face, force, honey, buck, ring, tooth, ship.
    *   Target Word: 'polish'.

2.  **Analyze the Target Word ('polish'):**
    *   Meaning 1: To make something smooth and shiny by rubbing (verb/noun). Associated with shoes, furniture, nails, silver, etc.
    *   Meaning 2: Refinement, sophistication, elegance (noun).
    *   Meaning 3: Related to Poland (adjective/noun) - e.g., Polish people, Polish currency (zloty), etc.

3.  **Evaluate Available Words against 'polish':**
    *   *mole*: Animal, spy, skin mark. Weak connection.
    *   *nail*: Fingernail/toenail. You can polish nails (nail polish). Strong connection (Meaning 1).
    *   *opera*: Art form. Weak connection.
    *   *saturn*: Planet. Weak connection.
    *   *sub*: Sandwich, submarine, substitute. Weak connection.
